skill.name: copilot-mcp-server
skill.description: Use GitHub Copilot MCP tools for code review, debugging, explanation, refactoring, test generation, and technical questions.
skill.summary: Access GitHub Copilot MCP tools directly from supported AI coding clients.
skill.author: Aykahshi

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby/shell backtick, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance findings are false positives from Markdown examples and ordinary descriptive text. The skill has legitimate elevated risk because it sends user prompts or code to GitHub Copilot MCP tools and documents broad delegated tool access with allowAllTools.

medium_findings.title: External AI Service Data Exposure
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to pass prompts, context, and code snippets to GitHub Copilot MCP tools. This is expected functionality, but users may disclose proprietary code or sensitive project context to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool parameters accept prompts, context, and code, and the notes state that Copilot subscription and API limits apply. This confirms external service use, although no malicious exfiltration intent is present.

medium_findings.title: Broad Delegated Tool Access Option
medium_findings.description: The skill documents an allowAllTools option and shows it enabled in an example. Enabling broad tool access can expand the actions available to the downstream Copilot agent beyond simple text analysis.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowAllTools parameter is explicitly documented and enabled in an example. The exact downstream tool set is not defined in this file, so the impact depends on the user's MCP configuration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Tool Examples Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer marked JavaScript fenced examples and MCP tool names as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These are documentation snippets for MCP calls, not executable shell commands in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All cited locations are inside Markdown code fences showing MCP invocation syntax or sample code for review. No actual script file or runtime command execution is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Keywords
low_findings.description: The static high-risk hits occur in descriptive text such as the front matter description, model guidance, and integration notes. No cryptographic function, hash algorithm, scanner, or reconnaissance command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain prose or parameter names, not implementation code. The scanner appears to have matched short ambiguous words rather than security-relevant operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Broad Tool Delegation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill exposes an allowAllTools option for Copilot MCP calls. Users should leave this disabled unless the downstream tool set is understood and trusted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The option and enabled example are directly documented. The precise risk depends on external MCP server configuration, so confidence is high for the pattern but moderate for impact.

content.value_statement: Developers often need focused AI help without switching workflows or writing custom MCP calls. This skill documents direct Copilot MCP tools for review, debugging, explanations, refactoring, tests, and general coding help.
actual_capabilities: Ask Copilot coding questions with optional context and model selection.
Request focused code reviews for security, performance, maintainability, and best practices.
Explain code snippets using Copilot MCP tools.
Debug code by sending the code, error message, and optional context.
Generate unit test suggestions for a chosen testing framework.
Start and inspect Copilot conversation sessions for related work.
limitations: Requires an active GitHub Copilot subscription and configured MCP server.
Usage may count against Copilot API or subscription limits.
The skill is documentation only; it does not include an MCP server implementation.
Sensitive code or secrets should not be sent unless allowed by policy.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Use focused Copilot review prompts to check a change for security, performance, and maintainability concerns.

use_cases.title: Debug an Error Quickly
use_cases.description: Send a failing snippet, error text, and context to receive likely causes and repair guidance.

use_cases.title: Generate Test Coverage Ideas
use_cases.description: Ask Copilot to propose unit tests for existing functions in a selected framework.

prompt_templates.title: Ask a Coding Question
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use for a focused technical answer.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Copilot to answer this coding question. Include the relevant context and ask for a concise explanation with practical next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Existing Code
prompt_templates.scenario: Understand unfamiliar source code before editing it.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Copilot explain tool on this code. Summarize what it does, list important inputs and outputs, and identify risky assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Check a proposed change before merging.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Copilot review tool on this change. Focus on security, performance, maintainability, and missed edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Refactor and Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow that combines refactoring and validation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Copilot to propose a refactor plan for this code, then generate test cases that would verify the new behavior.

output_examples.input: A developer asks Copilot to review an authentication change for security risks.
output_examples.output: The response identifies likely injection, credential handling, and session risks, then suggests targeted fixes and test cases.

output_examples.input: A developer sends an error message and a short description of the failing workflow.
output_examples.output: The response explains probable causes, ranks them by likelihood, and recommends specific debugging steps.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for tests for a utility function and names the preferred framework.
output_examples.output: The response proposes normal, boundary, and failure cases that match the requested testing framework.

best_practices: Provide the smallest useful code excerpt and explain the goal clearly.
Choose the model based on task complexity and expected response depth.
Disable broad tool access unless the MCP server configuration is trusted.
anti_patterns: Do not send secrets, tokens, or regulated data in prompts or code snippets.
Do not enable broad tool access for routine explanations or simple reviews.
Do not mix unrelated tasks in one Copilot session when traceability matters.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It documents how to call GitHub Copilot MCP tools for coding assistance from supported AI clients.

faq.question: Does it include an MCP server?
faq.answer: No. It assumes a working Copilot MCP server and client configuration already exist.

faq.question: Can it review code for security?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented review tool supports security-focused review when requested.

faq.question: Can it generate tests?
faq.answer: Yes. The test generation tool accepts code and an optional testing framework.

faq.question: What data may leave my environment?
faq.answer: Prompts, context, code snippets, and error messages may be sent to Copilot MCP services.

faq.question: Is broad tool access recommended?
faq.answer: No. Use broad tool access only when the MCP tools are trusted and the task requires it.
